text: Planning a trip always fills me with excitement. Last summer, I spent weeks researching destinations and booking flights to Japan. When I arrived, everything felt different—the streets were clean, the signs were unknown, and the language barrier was real. I struggled to order food at first, pointing at menus and smiling. But trying local dishes like ramen and sushi became my favorite part of the day. Exploring new cultures taught me patience and curiosity. Even simple interactions, like using hand gestures to buy a train ticket, became memorable. Although communication was hard, the kindness of strangers made every moment special. These experiences stay with me, reminding me that travel is about more than places—it’s about the memories we create along the way.
